title: Who Is Playing Who
description: Party speaks with the NPC who thinks they're the player. Meta beat. Careful, not heavy-handed.

steps.text: The Player sat at the edge of the plaza, hunched over nothing. Their hands moved as though they were holding a controller. They appeared to be in the middle of something important.

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: Oh! Hi. You again. I was grinding levels on this one spot. The enemies come back.

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: They respawn if you wait long enough. It's very efficient.

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: Well, of course not. I cleared them. They'll come back.

lines.speaker: Voice
lines.text: Do you ever leave this spot?

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: Why would I? The experience curve is optimal here.

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: I'm thirty levels above anything in this world. Eventually I'll be high enough for the postgame.

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: There always is. You just have to grind enough.

lines.speaker: Edge
lines.text: You know, I think we've met someone like you in every world.

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: That's the thing about players. We're consistent.

lines.speaker: The Player
lines.text: Good luck with whatever you're doing. Watch your step at the edges. They've been patching the collision.

steps.text: The Player returned to their task. Their hands kept moving, holding a controller that was not there, pressing buttons that did nothing, in a world that was slowly being optimized out from around them. They did not appear concerned. Grinding, after all, was consistent.
